root_text.ud1.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud1.6:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā mahākassapo pippaliguhāyaṁ viharati ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.ud1.6:1.4: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo aparena samayena tamhā ābādhā vuṭṭhāsi.
root_text.ud1.6:1.5: Atha kho āyasmato mahākassapassa tamhā ābādhā vuṭṭhitassa etadahosi:
root_text.ud1.6:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena pañcamattāni devatāsatāni ussukkaṁ āpannāni honti āyasmato mahākassapassa piṇḍapātapaṭilābhāya.
root_text.ud1.6:2.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo tāni pañcamattāni devatāsatāni paṭikkhipitvā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi—
root_text.ud1.6:2.4: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ rājagahe piṇḍāya carantaṁ yena daliddavisikhā kapaṇavisikhā pesakāravisikhā.
root_text.ud1.6:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud1.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud1.6:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Mahākassapa was staying in the Pipphali cave, and he was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.ud1.6:1.4: Then after some time he recovered from that illness.
translation_text.ud1.6:2.1: Now at that time five hundred deities were ready and eager for the chance to offer alms to Mahākassapa.
translation_text.ud1.6:2.2: But Mahākasspa turned down those deities. In the morning, he robed up, took his bowl and robe, and entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.ud1.6:2.3: He went to the streets of the poor, the destitute, and the embroiderers.
translation_text.ud1.6:2.4: The Buddha saw him wandering for alms in the streets of the poor, the destitute, and the embroiderers.
translation_text.ud1.6:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud1.6:4.3: with defilements ended and flaws purged:
translation_text.ud1.6:4.4: that’s who I declare a brahmin.”

variant_text.ud1.6:1.3: pippaliguhāyaṁ → pipphaliguhāyaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1); simbaliguhāyaṁ (mr) | ābādhiko → ābādhiko hoti (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
